title: Daily Market Digest: May 30, 2026
subtitle: Tech Sector Powers Gains Amid Shifting Sentiment

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Climb, Led by Tech as AI Demand Boosts Earnings
items.summary: U.S. equities closed higher on Friday, extending a record-setting rally, primarily driven by strong performances in the technology sector. Hopes for easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and impressive earnings reports from major tech firms, particularly those linked to artificial intelligence demand, fueled investor confidence. Despite these gains, overall market sentiment remains mixed, with some indicators suggesting potential overvaluation and consumer pessimism.

items.statContext: Up 0.22% on Friday, May 29. Gained 1.43% for the week and 6.4% month-to-date in May.
Up 0.20% on Friday, May 29. Rose 2.39% for the week and 8.36% month-to-date in May.
Up 0.72% on Friday, May 29. Climbed 0.9% for the week and 2.78% month-to-date in May.

items.headline: Dell Technologies
items.summary: Surged on robust Q1 earnings and heightened demand for AI computing.

items.headline: Snowflake
items.summary: Led weekly gains among US-listed stocks.

items.headline: Futu Holdings
items.summary: Declined significantly after its first-quarter earnings release.

items.headline: StepStone Group
items.summary: Among the significant laggards for the week.

items.headline: Technology Sector Continues to Lead Market Gains
items.summary: The technology sector remained the strongest performer, with a 19.79% surge in May, largely driven by the ongoing Artificial Intelligence rally. This concentration means a small group of large-cap tech stocks is heavily influencing the overall market indices.

items.headline: Financials Show Strength Amid Sector Rotation
items.summary: The financial sector saw gains for the week, up 0.56% on Friday, as traders rotated back into some lagging sectors. Shares of major investment banks like Goldman Sachs Group and Morgan Stanley reached all-time highs.

items.headline: Energy and Utilities Sectors Lag
items.summary: The Energy sector was among the worst performers, falling 5.6% in May and 5.31% for the week ending May 29. Utilities also lagged, experiencing a 4.35% loss month-to-date.

items.headline: Persistent Inflation Fuels Rate Hike Speculation
items.summary: The Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Index, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure, rose 3.8% year-over-year, indicating stubbornly elevated inflation. Core PCE increased by 3.3%. This suggests the Fed may remain cautious about interest rate cuts, and discussions about potential rate hikes, a significant policy shift, are gaining traction under new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Ease, Boosting Confidence
items.summary: Hopes for easing geopolitical tensions, particularly concerning the US-Iran conflict and prospects of a ceasefire agreement, contributed to a boost in investor confidence. This also led to a decline in crude oil prices, with Brent crude falling to $92.10 a barrel.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Hits New Low Amid Market Rally
items.summary: Despite the S&P 500 reaching new all-time highs, consumer sentiment has hit a new low this month, falling below its previous low in 2022. More investors are feeling pessimistic than optimistic, and the 'Buffett indicator' has reached a new all-time high of approximately 231%, signaling potential market overvaluation.

items.headline: Key Earnings Reports on the Horizon
items.summary: While many companies have already reported, investors are looking ahead to upcoming earnings. Five Below ($FIVE) is scheduled to report on June 2, 2026, with Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Palo Alto Networks, and Broadcom also slated for early June.
